caption: Il mio videomessaggio per il 50° anniversario della NIAF.
text: Buongiorno, buona serata a tutti. As Prime Minister of Italy, it is a genuine honor to pay my tribute for the 50th anniversary of the National Italian American Foundation. What a wonderful gathering of people that testify the wonderful friendship between Italy and the United States. I'm really sad not to be with you in person tonight. Believe me, when I say there is nowhere else I would rather be. But there are too many things to do here for the country we all love. 50 years. Think about it. For half a century, NAIF has been the unwavering voice, the beating heart, and the strong backbone of the Italian American community. And I want to start by offering my deepest congratulations to the President, the Chairman, the Board of Staff, and every single member who has built this incredible bridge between our two great nations. You deserve a big round of applause. We are here to celebrate the DNA of opportunity, a heritage forged in the toughest neighborhoods built on honest work and sustained by unshakable belief in family, faith, and the future. Your ancestors didn't just come to America. They helped build and shape today's America. And that tradition of excellence continues brilliantly to this day. Tonight, you are honoring an incredible group of individuals representing an exemplification of Italian and Italian American achievement. In fact, with so many CEOs and innovators here tonight, I think it's fair to say that a significant portion of Italy's GDP is sitting right there in the Washington Hilton. That's the kind of power that comes from Italian excellence meeting American opportunity. And speaking of excellence, we must recognize the Maestro among you, the recipient of the Lifetime Achievement Award in Entertainment, the voice of Italy to the world, my friend Andrea Bocelli. Maestro, I remember so clearly when you recently sang at the conclusion of the G7 Summit in Puglia. Your powerful voice reminded world leaders what true harmony sounds like. That same harmony is the fundament of the alliance between Italy and the United States. Our strength, the strength of the West, is born from unparalleled individual excellence, the singular powerful voice of freedom. But it is only perfected when the entire orchestra is playing together in flawless, powerful harmony. This heritage is why we must never lose sight of what unites us. I know that there are forces that seek to divide us, to redefine our history and to tear down our shared traditions. They call it woe culture. They seek to cancel our culture. They are wrong. To try to erase Columbus Day is not just an attack on a statue or a date on the calendar. It is an attempt to erase the foundational history of Italian-Americans and to deny their hard-fought place in the tapestry of this nation. We will not let them do it. Columbus Day is here to stay. Let me quote the beautiful proclamation that President Trump issued last week. He said as we celebrate Columbus' legacy, we also acknowledge the contributions of the countless Italian-Americans who, like him, have endlessly contributed to our culture and our way of life. To this day, the United States and Italy share a special bond rooted in the timeless values of faith, family and freedom. Well said, President Trump. Thank you. Together, the United States and Italy are not just allies. We are the two pillars of the free world. We stand shoulder to shoulder from the fields of industry to the front lines of freedom, ensuring that the ocean of opportunity remains wide open for the next 50 years. Thank you, Niamh, for 50 years of keeping that voice strong and that orchestra in harmony. May God bless the Italian-American community. May God bless Italy. And may God bless the United States of America. Viva l'Italia. Viva l'America.
